page: Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is a shared-core virtual machine belonging to the Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 65 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned at the lower end of the spectrum. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1260.88 and 2444.49 respectively, both placing in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show a sharp contrast between cached small-block reads at 177.44 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 12.18 GB/sec, reflecting the limitations of the shared-core architecture and memory bus constraints. Passmark database operations scored 386.17, and Redis SET operations reached 1.39 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high memory density. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can lead to variable performance under sustained loads, alongside the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:1. This server is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, development environments, small utility scripts, and low-traffic caching instances.
description: The Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is a shared-core virtual machine in the Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor with 2 vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 65 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results position this instance in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng CPU scores and uncached memory bandwidth tests falling in the bottom 10% to 25% range. Database operations and Redis SET performance also reflect its entry-level, shared-resource architecture. This server represents a highly cost-efficient option for non-demanding workloads where dedicated CPU performance is not required. It is best suited for basic web serving, development testing, lightweight utility scripts, and small-scale caching applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine with NVMe storage, designed for lightweight development, testing, and basic web serving workloads.

2.0 GB of system memory and 65 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Low-tier benchmark performance suitable for non-intensive tasks
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
